reflection:
Yesterday landed well. Pain 3, mood 7, sleep 8, wind-down all checked, and recovery is climbing for the third day in a row, 60 then 63 then 69. That isn't noise, that's the evening protocol actually working. Last meal 18:45, screens off by 22:00, Marcus Aurelius before bed. You woke at 4 again, but went back down until 5:40. Not a bad night.

The thing worth flagging is what you noticed yourself, that DNS is winding the pelvic floor up rather than calming it. You wrote it down, and you said the Parr release work matters more than ever. So today, when you do DNS, treat the Parr releases as non-optional on the back end. You already know.

Also worth saying out loud, the toilet thing. Intensity 5 before, 2 after. That's the thought record doing its job. You panicked, you fixed it yourself, you came down. That moves the story from someone who breaks things to someone who also fixes them.

Throat tightness ticked up to 1, and acidity sat at 4. Worth watching but do not chase them.

For today, just hold the 9:30 shutdown. The plan you wrote yourself was already the right one.
opener: recovery climbing third day, sleep 8/10, you protected the wind-down
focus_for_today: after DNS, do the Parr release work without negotiation
